'''DS-181-4''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|callsign]] of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[TIE pilot|pilot]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After distinguishing himself during the [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugation]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], DS-181-4 received a promotion to [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]], part of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]], and flew as [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Turr Phennir]]'s [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], DS-181-4 was present for [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] arrival aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], and he later participated in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biography==
[[File:Saber4.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Saber 4, the TIE/IN interceptor flown by DS-181-4]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] designated by the [[Callsign/Legends|callsign]] DS-181-4 was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[TIE pilot|pilot]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="CCG1" /> After scoring twelve [[Death/Legends|kills]] during the [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugation]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]],<ref name="CSWE-DS-181-4">{{CSWECite|I|212|DS-181-4}}</ref> DS-181-4 earned a position in [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel's]] [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]]—part of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]]<ref name="CCG1" />—[[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] [[Saber 4]].<ref name="CCG2">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 4}}</ref> Flying as the [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] of [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Turr Phennir]],<ref name="CCG1" /> DS-181-4 was often assigned to fly in a reserve position during battle.<ref name="CCG2" /> Preferring to fly in the 181st, DS-181-4 repeatedly refused promotions so that he could remain in the [[Squadron/Legends|unit]].<ref name="CCG1" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
[[File:DS-181-4.png|thumb|right|200px|DS-181-4 during the Galactic Civil War]]
Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], DS-181-4 partook in a skirmish against at least one [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]]. He was defeated in the fight but managed to escape in his crippled [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]]. DS-181-4 also participated in battles between [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces in the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo]], [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo]], [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian]], [[Dantooine system/Legends|Dantooine]], and [[Karthakk system]]s during the Galactic Civil War. These battles involved an Imperial [[Lancer-class frigate/Legends|''Lancer''-class frigate]] being pitted against a Rebel [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]], with numerous starfighters aiding in the defense or offense of the [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to the beginning of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> DS-181-4 was present when the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrived aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] to discuss the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] lagging construction schedule with the station's commander, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Tiaan Jerjerrod/Legends|Tiaan Jerjerrod]]. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] announced that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself would be arriving to personally oversee the remainder of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] assembly.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> DS-181-4 later flew on Phennir's wing during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="CSWE-DS-181-4" /> during which Phennir was assigned to protect the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']].<ref name="CCG3">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 2}}</ref> The battle ended in an Imperial defeat with the destruction of the Death Star.<ref name="CSWE-Battle of Endor">{{CSWECite|I|235–236|Endor, Battle of}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
DS-181-4's considerable skill as a TIE pilot earned him a place in the 181st Imperial Fighter Group.<ref name="CSWE-DS-181-4" /> Turning down multiple promotions, DS-181-4 preferred to remain as a pilot in the 181st.<ref name="CCG1" /> In battle, DS-181-4 often flew in a reserve position.<ref name="CCG2" /> He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]],<ref name="CCG1" /> brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="SWG" /> and fair skin.<ref name="CCG1" />

==Equipment==
DS-181-4 wore a black [[Imperial Navy crewman]]'s [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]] during his service in the Empire.<ref name="CCG1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:MoffJerjerrodGreetsVader.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The scene from the ''Special Edition'' of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' that was reused from Vader's arrival on the Death Star in ''Return of the Jedi'']]
DS-181-4 first appeared in the opening scene of [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] as an unnamed background character awaiting the arrival of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader on the second Death Star.<ref name="ROTJ" /> In [[2000]], he was first identified by name on a card for the ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]],<ref name="CCG1" /> with the name simultaneously being used on a separate card for his call sign.<ref name="CCG2" />

Because part of the scene showing Vader's arrival on the second Death Star in ''Return of the Jedi'' was reused in the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|''Special Edition'']] release of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] for a scene showing Vader arriving aboard the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] from [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], the actor who portrayed DS-181-4 can be seen in the ''Special Edition'' version of ''The Empire Strikes Back'', alongside other extras who portrayed [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] in ''Return of the Jedi''.<ref name="ROTJ" /><ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

===''Star Wars Galaxies''===
DS-181-4 was added to the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']] as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]]<ref name="SWG" /> in [[W:c:swg:Game Update 20|Game Update 20]].<ref name="Update 20">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=69207&month=082011|text=Game Update 20: Galactic Civil War 2 – Space}}</ref> Rebel players who requested [[W:c:swg:Tier|Tier 7]] [[W:c:swg:Destroy Duty Missions|Duty Missions]] from certain [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] had a chance to battle an [[W:c:swg:Ace Pilot System|elite Imperial pilot]] during the mission's boss stage. One of these pilots could be DS-181-4. If the player defeated DS-181-4, the Imperial was not destroyed. Instead, a message was displayed to the player, indicating the successful defeat of the pilot. The player then received a [[W:c:swg:Collection|collection]] icon that added up to award a [[W:c:swg:Badges|badge]] if the player succeeded in destroying all ten of the elite Imperial pilots.<ref name="SWG" />

However, elite Imperial pilots could also be found flying in various [[Space warfare/Legends|space battles]], which took place in the Naboo, Tatoo, Corellian, Dantooine, and Karthakk systems. Because DS-181-4 only appeared in the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] portion of ''Star Wars Galaxies'', originally added with the [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump to Lightspeed'']] expansion pack, he could normally only be seen inside his respective TIE interceptor. However, a [[Comm unit/Legends|comm]] display could appear during the battle, displaying DS-181-4's face. In addition, the collection icon depicted DS-181-4's physical appearance. While DS-181-4 piloted a TIE interceptor in the game, there is no indication it was Saber 4. Although players could choose not to participate in any of the battles or collections connected to DS-181-4,<ref name="SWG" /> this article assumes 100 percent game completion.
